title: A proof-of-concept preclinical study using a novel thermal iso-lation device in a porcine kidney auto-transplantation model
author: Ernst, Lisa
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Abstract:  Ischemic reperfusion injury is still remaining as a fundamental problem during organ transplantation. One key factor is the rapid rewarming during the time of vascular reconstruction. In this pilot study a new thermal isolation bag (TIB) for organ transplantation is to be tested to prevent organ rewarming. Isolation capacity, tissue compatibility, and usability were tested initially ex vivo on porcine kidneys (n=24) followed by first in vivo usage. 12 female German landrace pigs received a kidney auto-transplantation after 24h cold storage (4°C). During the implantation process the kidney was either isolated with the new TIB, or it was not thermos-protected at all. In this proof-of-concept study the usability (knife-to-skin-time) and the general thermal capacity (30 min. warm storage at 40°C ex vivo p<0.001) was shown. The clinical outcome showed significant differences in the determination of CRP and pi-GST levels. Start temperature at reperfusion and development within the first 100 sec. after reperfusion have shown a trend of slower increase (p<0.06). However, the effect on renal outcomes in not severely pre-damaged kidneys does not appear to be conclusively significant. A close follow up study is necessary, especially in the context of prolonged anastomosis- and transplantation-times related to surgical complications.

keywords: kidney transplantation; organ isolation; ischemic reperfusion injury
